orgDesign.companyProfile: Typical mid-size mobility and payments company spanning fleet services, merchant acceptance, energy transition, and shared payment operations. The organization is now modeled as a network of smaller teams sized around 10 FTE.
orgDesign.operatingModel: Business-line teams own customer and partner propositions, while shared risk and platform teams standardize controls and data. Larger value streams are split into sibling teams so accountability stays close to concrete capability ownership.
designPrinciples: Fleet, merchant, and energy workflows need separate business ownership.
Payments, identity, and risk capabilities should be centralized to control complexity.
Team structure is defined ahead of the more detailed customer and product-brick datasets for this domain.
orgDesign.staffingPrinciple: Prefer teams of roughly 8-11 FTE, with broader customer streams split into multiple sibling teams inside the same group or team family.
orgDesign.groupingPrinciple: Use top-level groups for major value streams and a second layer of subgroups for team families when a stream contains multiple 10-FTE teams.
orgDesign.groupLeadershipPrinciple: When a group contains several 10-FTE teams, add lightweight group-level leadership and principal IC roles rather than scaling any single team too far beyond its local scope.

groups.name: Business Lines / Fleet Mobility Solutions
groups.mission: Own customer propositions across the DKV mobility portfolio. This subgroup focuses on the Fleet Mobility Solutions team family.

teams.name: Fleet Mobility Solutions - Cell 1
teams.mission: Own fleet account management, card programs, and driver workflows for core fleet customers. This team specifically focuses on cell 1.

staffing.staffingGuidance: Each customer-facing team is intentionally kept near 10 FTE so it can own a narrow journey or capability slice with clear accountability and fast decision-making.

roles.responsibility: Own the roadmap, prioritization, and KPI tradeoffs for this team scope.
Lead delivery execution, staffing, and engineering health for the team.
Set technical direction and guard long-term architecture quality across the team scope.
Build and evolve backend services, business logic, and integration points.
Build user-facing workflows and maintain a high-quality interaction model.
Own regression depth, release confidence, and automated quality coverage.
Shape the end-to-end workflow, information architecture, and UX quality.
Bridge product decisions with domain semantics, data quality, and operational fit.

groups.name: Shared Platform / Payments & Risk Platform
groups.mission: Provide payment, data, and control capabilities used across propositions. This subgroup focuses on the Payments & Risk Platform team family.

teams.name: Payments & Risk Platform - Cell 1
teams.mission: Own payment processing, fraud controls, settlement, and identity capabilities shared across DKV products. This team specifically focuses on cell 1.

staffing.staffingGuidance: Each platform team is intentionally kept near 10 FTE so ownership stays coherent while shared services remain dependable for multiple stream-aligned teams.

roles.responsibility: Own the roadmap, prioritization, and KPI tradeoffs for this team scope.
Lead delivery execution, staffing, and engineering health for the team.
Set technical direction and guard long-term architecture quality across the team scope.
Build reusable platform capabilities, internal tooling, and service foundations.
Own reliability, observability, incident readiness, and operational resilience.
Provide durable pipelines, canonical data models, and analytics-ready datasets.
Own regression depth, release confidence, and automated quality coverage.
Embed control design, auditability, and secure-by-default practices.
